channel.title: best-practices on PowerShell.org - Welcome Automaters!
channel.description: Recent content in best-practices on PowerShell.org - Welcome Automaters!

item.title: Advanced PowerShell Error Handling
item.description: Error handling is one of the most important aspects of writing robust PowerShell scripts. In this article, we’ll explore advanced techniques to handle errors gracefully. Error Action Preferences PowerShell gives you control over how errors are handled through error action preferences: Stop: Treat errors as terminating Continue: Ignore the error and continue (default) SilentlyContinue: Suppress error output Inquire: Prompt the user when an error occurs Ignore: Same as SilentlyContinue Try-Catch-Finally Blocks The foundation of error handling in PowerShell:
